issues.description: ban/kick emits space:error but leaves the target sockets fully authorized until the 250ms delayed disconnect. During that window the socket still has socket.data.spaceId, role, and restriction unchanged, so a malicious client can keep sending chat/admin/editor/media events; existing handlers authorize from those cached fields and do not re-check the DB.
issues.recommendation: Revoke server-side state synchronously before the grace delay: mark the socket as no longer in-space, downgrade role/restriction, leave the room or run presence cleanup immediately, and make handlers reject revoked sockets. Then delay only the physical disconnect so the error packet can flush.

issues.description: Multi-tab ban/kick schedules disconnect for every socket, and each disconnect runs leaveSpace, which deletes the same userId and emits player:left. That produces duplicate player:left events and can also remove presence as soon as the first tab disconnects, before the remaining tab disconnect handlers run.
issues.recommendation: Make presence cleanup user-scoped rather than socket-count blind: either disconnect/cleanup once per user or have leaveSpace emit player:left only when no remaining socket for that user is still in the space room.

issues.description: The body limit is documented as bytes, but enforcement accumulates Buffer chunks into a string and checks raw.length. Multi-byte UTF-8 bodies can exceed 4096 bytes while staying under the character count, so the limit is not exact.
issues.recommendation: Track byte length from Buffer.byteLength/chunk.length while collecting the body, and reject when accumulated bytes exceed ENFORCE_MAX_BODY_BYTES.
issues.deferRationale: The endpoint is HMAC-protected and the practical bypass is small, so this is hardening rather than an immediate functional blocker.
